reason:
Both answers correctly recommend "fix, don't kill," cite the ~2.2:1 LTV:CAC ratio as thin-but-workable, and give a similar 60-90 day kill trigger tied to availability of cheaper scalable channels. Both list negative keywords/campaign pruning and landing-page/funnel improvements as fixes — solid, correct answers overall.

Answer B explicitly covers the branded-vs-generic split (a named key point) and gives a sharper blended-CAC insight (the $900 blended average likely hides a $500 winner and a $1,500 loser), which better addresses the "blended vs paid CAC" point than A does. B also adds a valuable, correct piece of arithmetic — LTV/$150 implies a ~13-month average customer lifetime and ~7.5% monthly churn — surfacing churn reduction as a lever that may be higher-leverage than ad optimization, something A never mentions. A's unique contributions (fixing conversion tracking to closed-won/revenue-based bidding, raising LTV via annual prepay) are also solid, but A omits the branded/generic split and blended-CAC decomposition entirely.

Neither answer meaningfully develops the "compare against content/SEO for compounding returns" point beyond a passing mention as an alternative channel, so that's roughly a wash.

On balance, B hits more of the specified key points and adds a genuinely useful, correct insight (churn math), making it marginally more complete and decision-relevant without added length or hedging.</parameter>
</invoke>
